schemaVersion: Ehel English Course Final Assessment v1.2
subject: English
title: Grade 4 English Final Course Quiz
description: A cumulative assessment of vocabulary, reading, language and communication across Units 1-9.

sections.title: Words and Meaning
sections.description: Use Grade 4 vocabulary accurately in familiar and new contexts.

sections.title: Reading and Language
sections.description: Show reading understanding and control of Grade 4 language patterns.

sections.title: Communication and Application
sections.description: Apply English to speaking, writing and purposeful communication.

questions.question: What does 'mail' mean?
questions.options: A person who belongs to a country and has rights there. | Letters and packages that are sent or delivered. | To have the same opinion, or to say yes to something. | To keep doing something without stopping.
questions.correctAnswer: Letters and packages that are sent or delivered.
questions.explanation: I received a letter in the mail today.

questions.question: What does 'language' mean?
questions.options: A way people talk and write to share ideas and feelings. | To get something you want, or to become better at something. | To have the same opinion, or to say yes to something. | To keep doing something without stopping.
questions.correctAnswer: A way people talk and write to share ideas and feelings.
questions.explanation: English is a language that many people speak around the world.

questions.question: What does 'citizen' mean?
questions.options: A person who belongs to a country and has rights there. | The hard work you put into doing something. | To get something you want, or to become better at something. | To keep doing something without stopping.
questions.correctAnswer: A person who belongs to a country and has rights there.
questions.explanation: Every citizen has rights and rules to follow.

questions.question: What does 'agree' mean?
questions.options: The hard work you put into doing something. | To get something you want, or to become better at something. | To have the same opinion, or to say yes to something. | To learn something very well.
questions.correctAnswer: To have the same opinion, or to say yes to something.
questions.explanation: I agree with you that reading is fun.

questions.question: What does 'breath' mean?
questions.options: A small amount of water in the air or on a surface. | The air you take in and let out of your lungs. | The soft hair that covers some animals' bodies. | The top or outside part of something.
questions.correctAnswer: The air you take in and let out of your lungs.
questions.explanation: I took a deep breath before I started to speak.

questions.question: What does 'sweat' mean?
questions.options: A deep valley with steep sides, often with a river at the bottom. | The clear water that comes from your skin when you are hot. | The soft hair that covers some animals' bodies. | The top or outside part of something.
questions.correctAnswer: The clear water that comes from your skin when you are hot.
questions.explanation: After running in the sun, I wiped the sweat from my face.

questions.question: What does 'moisture' mean?
questions.options: A deep valley with steep sides, often with a river at the bottom. | A large field of grass and flowers where animals graze. | A small amount of water in the air or on a surface. | The soft hair that covers some animals' bodies.
questions.correctAnswer: A small amount of water in the air or on a surface.
questions.explanation: The grass was wet with moisture after the rain.

questions.question: What does 'surface' mean?
questions.options: A deep valley with steep sides, often with a river at the bottom. | A large field of grass and flowers where animals graze. | A part of the sea or a lake that is partly surrounded by land. | The top or outside part of something.
questions.correctAnswer: The top or outside part of something.
questions.explanation: The surface of the lake was calm and shiny.

questions.question: What does 'sandwich' mean?
questions.options: A part of a plant, like pepper, that adds flavour to food. | A place where bread and cakes are made and sold. | Food made of two pieces of bread with meat, cheese, or vegetables inside. | Small white or brown grains that people cook and eat.
questions.correctAnswer: Food made of two pieces of bread with meat, cheese, or vegetables inside.
questions.explanation: I ate a chicken sandwich for lunch.

questions.question: What does 'lamb' mean?
questions.options: A part of a plant, like pepper, that adds flavour to food. | A place where bread and cakes are made and sold. | A young sheep, or the meat from it. | New and clean; not old or spoiled.
questions.correctAnswer: A young sheep, or the meat from it.
questions.explanation: We cooked lamb and rice for the guests.

questions.question: What does 'rice' mean?
questions.options: A place where bread and cakes are made and sold. | Hard to bite, so you need to chew it a lot. | New and clean; not old or spoiled. | Small white or brown grains that people cook and eat.
questions.correctAnswer: Small white or brown grains that people cook and eat.
questions.explanation: She ate rice with her chicken.

questions.question: What does 'spice' mean?
questions.options: A part of a plant, like pepper, that adds flavour to food. | Hard to bite, so you need to chew it a lot. | New and clean; not old or spoiled. | Very dangerous; able to cause death.
questions.correctAnswer: A part of a plant, like pepper, that adds flavour to food.
questions.explanation: We added some spice to make the soup tastier.

questions.question: What does 'service' mean?
questions.options: An idea or opinion in your mind. | Facts or details about something. | Help or work done for others. | To find or learn something new.
questions.correctAnswer: Help or work done for others.
questions.explanation: The library started a free book service for the town.

questions.question: What does 'deliver' mean?
questions.options: An idea or opinion in your mind. | To bring something to a person or place. | To find or learn something new. | What you know or learn.
questions.correctAnswer: To bring something to a person or place.
questions.explanation: The mail carrier will deliver our letters.

questions.question: What does 'information' mean?
questions.options: Facts or details about something. | Something difficult; or, to test someone. | To find or learn something new. | What you know or learn.
questions.correctAnswer: Facts or details about something.
questions.explanation: The website has useful information about our town.

questions.question: What does 'thought' mean?
questions.options: An idea or opinion in your mind. | Something difficult; or, to test someone. | Something more important than other things. | What you know or learn.
questions.correctAnswer: An idea or opinion in your mind.
questions.explanation: She shared her thought about the new plan.

questions.question: What does 'gallop' mean?
questions.options: A shape that curls round and round, moving out from the centre. | How fast something happens, or how much of it there is. | To go forward or to keep going. | To run fast on four legs, like a horse.
questions.correctAnswer: To run fast on four legs, like a horse.
questions.explanation: The horse began to gallop across the field.

questions.question: What does 'accelerate' mean?
questions.options: A shape that curls round and round, moving out from the centre. | How fast something happens, or how much of it there is. | To go faster, or to make something go faster. | To look at something quickly or secretly.
questions.correctAnswer: To go faster, or to make something go faster.
questions.explanation: The car can accelerate when the driver presses the pedal.

questions.question: What does 'proceed' mean?
questions.options: How fast something happens, or how much of it there is. | To go forward or to keep going. | To look at something for a long time. | To look at something quickly or secretly.
questions.correctAnswer: To go forward or to keep going.
questions.explanation: After the bell rings, we will proceed to the playground.

questions.question: What does 'spiral' mean?
questions.options: A shape that curls round and round, moving out from the centre. | To look at something for a long time. | To look at something quickly or secretly. | To look at something to make sure it is correct or safe.
questions.correctAnswer: A shape that curls round and round, moving out from the centre.
questions.explanation: The snail moved in a spiral on the ground.

questions.question: What does 'janitor' mean?
questions.options: A person who buys and sells things, often in a market. | A person who cleans and takes care of a building. | A person who designs or builds machines, roads, or buildings. | A person who does hard work, often with their hands.
questions.correctAnswer: A person who cleans and takes care of a building.
questions.explanation: The janitor keeps our school clean and tidy.

questions.question: What does 'carpenter' mean?
questions.options: A person who builds things from wood. | A person who buys and sells things, often in a market. | A person who designs or builds machines, roads, or buildings. | A person who leads and makes decisions for a city or region.
questions.correctAnswer: A person who builds things from wood.
questions.explanation: The carpenter made a beautiful wooden table.

questions.question: What does 'laborer' mean?
questions.options: A person chosen to help make the laws for a country. | A person who buys and sells things, often in a market. | A person who does hard work, often with their hands. | A person who leads and makes decisions for a city or region.
questions.correctAnswer: A person who does hard work, often with their hands.
questions.explanation: The laborer helped build the school walls.

questions.question: What does 'engineer' mean?
questions.options: A person chosen to help make the laws for a country. | A person who designs or builds machines, roads, or buildings. | A person who helps people understand and follow the law. | A person who leads and makes decisions for a city or region.
questions.correctAnswer: A person who designs or builds machines, roads, or buildings.
questions.explanation: The engineer drew plans for a new bridge.

questions.question: What does 'nervous' mean?
questions.options: Feeling worried or a little afraid about something that might happen. | Not sure that something is true or will happen. | Really wanting to know or learn something. | Very, very scared.
questions.correctAnswer: Feeling worried or a little afraid about something that might happen.
questions.explanation: I felt nervous before my big test at school.

questions.question: What does 'anxious' mean?
questions.options: Feeling happy about something you did or who you are. | Feeling very worried, with lots of worried thoughts. | Not sure that something is true or will happen. | Really wanting to know or learn something.
questions.correctAnswer: Feeling very worried, with lots of worried thoughts.
questions.explanation: She was anxious about being in the dark.

questions.question: What does 'terrified' mean?
questions.options: Feeling happy about something you did or who you are. | Kind and soft in the way you act; not rough or loud. | Really wanting to know or learn something. | Very, very scared.
questions.correctAnswer: Very, very scared.
questions.explanation: I was terrified when I saw the huge spider.

questions.question: What does 'doubtful' mean?
questions.options: Feeling happy about something you did or who you are. | Kind and soft in the way you act; not rough or loud. | Not sure that something is true or will happen. | Showing good manners and being kind to others.
questions.correctAnswer: Not sure that something is true or will happen.
questions.explanation: He felt doubtful about winning the game.

questions.quizTitle: Tools, Machines, and Everyday Items checkpoint
questions.question: What does 'equipment' mean?
questions.options: A flat object that protects you from something harmful. | A strong bag used to carry papers and important work things. | A tool that holds pieces of paper together with a small metal clip. | Things that are used for a special job or task, like tools or machines.
questions.correctAnswer: Things that are used for a special job or task, like tools or machines.
questions.explanation: We need special equipment to play football, like a ball and goalposts.

questions.quizTitle: Tools, Machines, and Everyday Items checkpoint
questions.question: What does 'folder' mean?
questions.options: A cover or container used to hold papers and keep them tidy. | A flat object that protects you from something harmful. | A tool that holds pieces of paper together with a small metal clip. | The solid tools, parts, or machines you can touch and use to build or fix things.
questions.correctAnswer: A cover or container used to hold papers and keep them tidy.
questions.explanation: I put my homework in my folder so it does not get lost.
